figures.caption: Figure 4: Pareto fronts of the search space of all 15 router-design pairs in ASAP7. Selected router QoR is reported in Table 2.
Table 2: QoRMetrics post GR and post DR of selected evolved router for ASAP7. Positive ΔWL denotes reduction.
Figure 7: Base CUGR uses one sparse-grid configuration, while CUGR_AES evaluates multiple candidate grids per net and keeps the best-scoring route tree.

Figure 5: Pareto fronts of the search space for 20 of the 21 router-design pairs in Nangate45 (NG45); SPR_BP is omitted (but can be found on our repository [10]). QoR metrics of the selected router for all 21 pairs are reported in Table 3.
Figure 3: Knowledge base and context provided to GR-Evolve.
Table 4: QoRMetrics post GR and post DR of selected evolved router for SKY130HD. Positive ΔWL denotes reduction.

Figure 6: Pareto fronts of the evolution search space for 8 of the 9 router-design pairs in SKY130HD; SPR_JPEG is omitted (but can be found on our repository [10]). QoR metrics for the selected routers are reported in Table 4.
Table 3: QoRMetrics post GR and post DR of selected evolved router for Nangate45. Positive ΔWL denotes a reduction.
